source: DFM AI Tool Wish List — Consultant Brief (DOCX); input/output from brief

output: Predicted retention curve (similar to YouTube Analytics)
Improvement suggestions including opening hook with rationale

input: List of ~20 competitor channel URLs per client
output: Analysis of why they outperformed (theme, format, timing)
Categorized by theme, format, and timing patterns
Score and critique (e.g. Slack command response)
Thumbnail text candidates from script (curiosity-gap phrases)
Cross-reference with Google Trends/SEO for search demand
Slack alerts to CD with before/after and delta

input: Context: brand voice, past scripts, performance data, style guides
output: Consistent ideation (Claude Project or Notion/Slack-integrated bot)

input: Client voice examples (previous scripts, cadence, vocabulary)
output: Rewritten script matching client voice and delivery style

input: Title or hook (e.g. posted in Slack)

output: Per-client dashboard of outlier content in their niche

input: Frame.io comments (per video asset and/or per client)
output: Alert when sentiment drops below threshold (Slack or dashboard)

input: Client-specific QC checklist (intro/outro, lower-third, audio, color, b-roll, captions, export settings)
output: Reduces manual QC burden and revision cycles

input: Slack messages, email threads, Frame.io comments (client-facing)
output: Flags: shifts in tone, emerging frustrations, positive signals
Privacy and data handling per scope

input: Template key (kickoff, status, revision, reminder, check-in)
output: Templated Slack message with placeholders filled
Optional: Slack Workflow Builder or bot trigger on schedule

input: Natural language command (e.g. via Slack or chat)
Example: "Create task for Mango Pod, longform edit for Client Alpha, due Friday, assign to Editor 1"
output: Proposed ClickUp task (list, name, due date, assignee, fields, tags)
Task created in ClickUp after confirm
